name: Blind Date Cookies
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup light brown sugar, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Adjust a rack to the top position.
Cut parchment paper to fit cookie sheets.
Stuff each date with one walnut half or a few pieces of walnut, then close the date around the nut. Set aside.
In a bowl, sift together flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
Cream butter until smooth, add vanilla extract, and beat in sugar until well combined. Add egg and beat thoroughly. Gradually mix in half the dry ingredients on low speed, then all sour cream, followed by remaining dry ingredients. Scrape bowl after each addition and beat only until combined. Transfer to a shallow bowl for easier handling.
Using two forks, drop each stuffed date into the dough and roll around until fully coated. Space cookies 2-3 inches apart on prepared sheets.
Bake one sheet at a time for about 10 minutes or until lightly browned. While baking, prepare glaze by melting butter and mixing with sugar, vanilla extract, and milk to soft mayonnaise consistency. Keep covered when not in use.
Remove from oven; brush cookies immediately with glaze using a pastry brush. Transfer to rack to cool until glaze is set.
